name: E2E: each scraped rule is tagged with the industry four-axis canonical vocabulary (Linguist / IaC dialect / prose), not CTP-invented strings
command: bash "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/commands/classify-rule.sh" --title 'Use const not var' --prose 'Use const in TypeScript .ts files.' --json > ts.json 2>/dev/null && bash "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/commands/classify-rule.sh" --title 'Pin versions' --prose 'Terraform .tf must pin required_version.' --json > tf.json 2>/dev/null && bash "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/commands/classify-rule.sh" --title 'Record ADR' --prose 'Design decisions are recorded as an architecture decision record.' --json > adr.json 2>/dev/null && node -e 'const ts=require(process.cwd()+"/ts.json"),tf=require(process.cwd()+"/tf.json"),adr=require(process.cwd()+"/adr.json");if(!ts.applies_to.linguist_aliases.includes("typescript"))process.exit(1);if(!tf.applies_to.iac_dialects.includes("terraform"))process.exit(1);if(adr.applies_to_prose!==true)process.exit(1)' && echo e2e2-four-axis-tagged 1>&2
